implementation_scope: Develop backend and UI components for AI interpretation to create a background task (D001).
Implement a notification system for AI interpretation task completion (D002).
Integrate two image sources for AI interpretation: large-scale batch from Vendor A and user-uploaded individual images (D004).
Develop UI/API for users to confirm or deny suspected points after AI interpretation (D005).
Implement functionality for users to place confirmed suspected points under monitoring (D006).
Add fields for 'reason' and implement polygon upload for monitoring (D007).
Implement polygon validation logic to accept large-area polygons and filter out overly small areas during monitoring polygon upload (D008, D009).
Develop an image upload duplicate-prevention mechanism for large image files (D010).
Modify the two-period image comparison feature to no longer pull images from the existing image library (D011).
Implement manual annotation functionality for the two-period image comparison feature (D012).
Develop map viewer functionality to load historically uploaded imagery (D016).
Implement map viewer filters for imagery list by date, image-name keyword, and type (satellite, aerial photo, drone) (D017, D018, D019).
Implement map viewer functionality to display points on the map (D020).
Add a 'locate' button for points on the map, which zooms the map to the point's location (D021, D022).
Implement automatic reversion of displayed imagery to point display when the map is zoomed out past a specified scale (D023).
Implement map view locking and disable panning in comparison mode (D024).
Add a 'release drag mode' button to restore panning functionality when the map view is locked (D025).
Implement functionality for users to adjust image transparency in the map viewer (D026).
Implement project organization by year (D028).
Implement functionality for creating suspected points within a project (D029).
Enforce requirement for an uploaded polygon when creating suspected points (D031).
Ensure suspected point creation does not require image uploads (D032).
Implement yearly layering for suspected points (D033).
Develop and add a 'New Survey Point Data' interface (D034).

Implement specific fields for the 'New Survey Point Data' interface: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method, volume, area, section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location (D035, D036, D037).
Plan and execute migration of the legacy MS file management system to the new system (D038).
Redesign the user interface for the migrated MS file management system while preserving its original functionality (D039, D040).
Re-implement or integrate the existing email-based follow-up/reminder feature (D043).
Integrate selected suspected points into the follow-up module (D044).
Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g., showing days remaining) to the follow-up module (D045).
Add a follow-up dispatch interface, leveraging existing draft designs (D046).
Implement connection between follow-up actions and survey-report management (D047).
Develop survey-report management with fields for time, description, action taken, and uploaded images (D048).
Prepare a data-flow document detailing post-AI interpretation data movement (image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database) (D049, D050, D056).

constraints.text: AI interpretation model execution must be set up to run on an on-site workstation (D013).
Images for model execution will be sent to the client's Access Point (AP), which then calls the on-site workstation (D014).
The base map for the system must be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D015).
The map viewer's design must reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D027).
The legacy MIS system migration is mandated by information-security compliance requirements (D041).
The project is expected to kick off in mid-May if the bidding process is successful (D052).
Immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation may be needed on the afternoon of bid award day (D053).
Sherry is responsible for finishing the 'add suspected point' data interface by May 18th (D054).
Sherry is responsible for preparing the data-flow document for A-Bao to review by May 18th (D056).
Consolidated existing interfaces, system architecture diagram, and data flow diagram are required for the requirements interview (D061, D062, D063).

open_technical_questions: What are the specific technical details of the AI interpretation notification mechanism (e.g., protocol, payload, delivery channels)? (D003)
How should suspected point layers be technically structured, specifically regarding splitting by county/city? This impacts data modeling and GIS integration. (D030)
What are the detailed technical requirements for the legacy MIS system migration, including data mapping, schema transformations, and the chosen migration strategy? (D042)
What are the specific technical configurations for who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule (e.g., user roles, scheduling logic, notification delivery mechanisms)? (D064)

acceptance_criteria: Given an AI interpretation run is initiated, Then exactly one task shall appear in the background task list.
Given an AI interpretation task has completed, Then a notification shall be sent to the user.
Given the AI interpretation feature, Then users shall be able to initiate interpretation using imagery from Vendor A (batch) or individual user-uploaded images.
Given AI interpretation has completed and generated a list of suspected points, When the user reviews the list, Then the user shall be able to explicitly confirm or deny each suspected point.
Given a suspected point has been confirmed by the user, When the user reviews the confirmed point, Then the user shall be able to choose to place that point under watch/monitoring.

Given a user attempts to place a confirmed suspected point under monitoring, When the user proceeds, Then the system shall require the user to provide a reason and upload a polygon before monitoring can be initiated.
Given a user is uploading a polygon for monitoring, When a large-area polygon is provided, Then the system shall accept the large-area polygon as valid.
Given a user is uploading a polygon for monitoring, When an overly small polygon is provided, Then the system shall automatically filter out and reject the overly small polygon.
Given a user attempts to upload an image, When the exact same image file (based on content/hash) has been previously uploaded, Then the system shall prevent the duplicate upload and inform the user.
Given the two-period image comparison feature, Then the system shall not allow images to be selected from the existing image library for this feature.
Given the two-period image comparison feature, Then the user shall be able to manually annotate images for comparison.
Given the map viewer is opened, Then it shall successfully load and display imagery that was previously uploaded to the system.
Given a list of imagery in the map viewer, When the user applies a date filter, Then only imagery matching the specified date range shall be displayed.
Given a list of imagery in the map viewer, When the user applies an image-name keyword filter, Then only imagery with names matching the keyword shall be displayed.
Given a list of imagery in the map viewer, When the user applies a filter for image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone), Then only imagery of the selected type(s) shall be displayed.
Given the map viewer is displaying imagery, Then it shall also display suspected points on the map.
Given a point is selected or opened on the map viewer, Then a "locate" button shall be visible for that point.
Given a point is selected on the map viewer, When the "locate" button for that point is pressed, Then the map shall automatically zoom to the specific location of that point.
Given the map viewer is displaying imagery, When the user zooms out past a predefined scale, Then the displayed imagery shall automatically switch to a point-only display.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, Then the user shall not be able to pan the map view.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode with panning locked, Then a "release drag mode" button shall be available, and clicking it shall restore panning functionality.
Given an image is displayed in the map viewer, Then the user shall be able to adjust its transparency level.
Given a list of projects in the system, Then they shall be logically organized and viewable by year.
Given a project exists, Then users shall be able to create new suspected points and associate them with that specific project.
Given a user attempts to create a new suspected point, When the user proceeds, Then the system shall require an uploaded polygon for the suspected point to be created.
Given a user is creating a new suspected point, Then the system shall not require or prompt for an image upload for the suspected point itself.
Given suspected points are displayed on the map, Then all suspected points created within a single year shall belong to and be displayed as a single layer.
Given the suspected point management section, Then a "New Survey Point Data" interface/option shall be accessible.
Given the "New Survey Point Data" interface, Then it shall include a "drawing number" input field.
Given the "New Survey Point Data" interface, Then it shall include a "key-monitoring-zone" input field.

Given the "New Survey Point Data" interface, Then it shall contain all the following fields: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.
Given the legacy MS file management system's functionality is migrated, Then its user interface shall reflect the new system's design standards.
Given the legacy MS file management system's functionality is migrated, Then all functionalities present in the original system shall operate identically in the new system.
Given suspected points are created, Then users shall be able to select specific suspected points and add them as items to the follow-up module.
Given the follow-up module is accessed, Then a schedule-tracking interface showing relevant details (e.g., days remaining) shall be displayed for selected follow-up items.
Given the follow-up module, Then a "follow-up dispatch" interface shall be accessible.
Given a follow-up action is completed, Then the system shall link this action to the survey-report management module.
Given the survey-report management module is accessed, Then it shall include fields for recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images for each report.
test_scenarios: Successfully initiate AI interpretation using Vendor A batch imagery and verify exactly one task is created in the background task list and a notification is sent upon completion.
Successfully initiate AI interpretation using a user-uploaded image and verify exactly one task is created in the background task list and a notification is sent upon completion.
After AI interpretation completes, confirm some suspected points and deny others via the user interface, then verify the system reflects these choices correctly.
Confirm a suspected point, then successfully place it under monitoring by providing a valid reason and uploading a valid large-area polygon. Verify the point is added to monitoring.
Attempt to place a confirmed suspected point under monitoring without providing a reason; verify the system displays an error and prevents monitoring.
Attempt to place a confirmed suspected point under monitoring without uploading a polygon; verify the system displays an error and prevents monitoring.
Upload an image file, then attempt to upload the exact same image file again; verify the system prevents the duplicate upload and provides an informative message.

Load historical imagery in the map viewer, then apply filters by date, image-name keyword, and image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone) sequentially and in combination; verify correct imagery is displayed for each filter.
Open the map viewer, select a point, click the "locate" button, and verify the map automatically zooms to that point's location.
Enter map comparison mode, attempt to pan the map and verify it is locked. Click the "release drag mode" button and verify panning functionality is restored.
Load an image in the map viewer, adjust its transparency from 0% (fully transparent) to 100% (fully opaque) and back; observe and verify the visual changes.
Create a new suspected point within an existing project, ensuring a valid polygon is uploaded, and verify the point is saved successfully without requiring an image upload.

Access the "New Survey Point Data" interface, fill in all specified fields (survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method, volume, area, section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location) with valid data, and successfully create a new survey point.
Perform end-to-end functional tests on the migrated MS file management system to ensure all original functionalities operate identically in the new system, despite interface redesign.
Select multiple suspected points from the main view and add them to the follow-up module; verify they appear in the schedule-tracking interface with accurate 'days remaining' calculations.

Complete a follow-up action for a suspected point, then navigate to the survey-report management module and verify a new report is created with fields for time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
edge_cases: Attempt to upload a polygon for monitoring that is exactly at the defined threshold for being "overly small"; verify it is rejected.
Upload a polygon for monitoring that is at the maximum permissible area; verify it is accepted and functions correctly.
Upload an image, then upload an identical image file but with a different filename; verify the duplicate prevention mechanism still triggers correctly.
Upload an image, then upload a different image file but with the same filename; verify it is treated as a new, unique image and is uploaded successfully.
Zoom the map viewer precisely to the scale threshold where imagery is expected to revert to point display; verify the transition occurs smoothly and correctly.
Attempt to create a suspected point without providing any polygon data; verify the system rejects the creation with an appropriate error message.

For the "New Survey Point Data" interface, attempt to save a new survey point without filling the 'drawing number' or 'key-monitoring-zone' fields (if they are mandatory), and verify validation errors.

Input boundary values (minimum and maximum allowed) for numeric fields such as 'volume', 'area', 'X/Y coordinates', and 'longitude/latitude' in the "New Survey Point Data" interface; verify values are accepted/rejected as per design.
Test concurrency: multiple users attempting to initiate AI interpretation runs simultaneously or upload images at the same time; verify task creation and duplicate prevention remain consistent.
unresolved_behaviors: Clarify the specific mechanism for AI interpretation task completion notifications (e.g., in-app alert, email, push notification, content, and recipient details).
Confirm whether suspected point layers should be split by county/city for display and management in the system.

Clarify specific details regarding the legacy MIS system migration, especially regarding what constitutes "unchanged functionality" and any potential data mapping complexities or specific data migration verification steps.
Clarify who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule (e.g., specific roles, configurable timings, escalation paths, content of notifications).

decision_history: The AI interpretation feature will generate one background task per run, and a notification will be sent upon its completion.
The system will support two primary image sources for AI interpretation: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch from Vendor A, and individual images uploaded by users.
After AI interpretation, users will confirm suspected points (yes/no) and can then decide to place these confirmed points under monitoring.
Placing a point under monitoring requires the user to provide a reason and upload a polygon. Large-area polygons are valid, while overly small areas will be automatically filtered out.
A duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed for image uploads to handle large file sizes and prevent redundant uploads.
The two-period image comparison feature will no longer pull images from the existing image library; users will instead manually annotate images for this functionality.
The map viewer must be capable of loading historically uploaded imagery and filtering it by date, image-name keyword, and image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone).

The map viewer will display points on the map, and an 'open point' action will reveal a 'locate' button that automatically zooms the map to that location. When zoomed out past a certain scale, displayed imagery will revert to a point display.

In comparison mode, the map view will be locked and not pannable; a 'release drag mode' button will be provided to restore panning. Users will also be able to adjust image transparency, which is important for orthophoto analysis.
The map viewer's design will reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.

Projects within the system will be organized by year. Suspected points can be created within a project, and each year will form one layer for these points. Suspected points require an uploaded polygon, but no image upload is needed.

A 'New Survey Point Data' interface needs to be added, including fields such as survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method, volume, area, section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.

The legacy MS file management system will be migrated to the new system primarily to meet information-security compliance requirements. The interface for this system will be redesigned, but its core functionality will remain unchanged.

The new system will integrate a follow-up/reminder module, allowing selection of items for follow-up from suspected points. This module needs a schedule-tracking interface (e.g., showing days remaining) and a follow-up dispatch interface (for which Shao-Chien has a first draft).
After a follow-up action, the process will connect to survey-report management, which includes fields for time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.

A-Bao requested a data-flow document to explain data movement post-AI interpretation, specifically covering the flow from image source to interpretation task execution (workstation), result output, and finally to the monitoring database.
The next meeting, scheduled for 2026-05-15, will focus on discussing system architecture. The first requirements interview, scheduled for 2026-05-20, will cover initial requirements.
dependencies: Client Infrastructure: On-site workstation setup required to run the image comparison model (D013).
Client Infrastructure: Client's Access Point (AP) integration required to call the on-site workstation for image processing (D014).
External Provider: Base map provision from GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D015).
Design Reference: Map viewer design to reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D027).
Internal Deliverable: Shao-Chien's first draft of the follow-up dispatch interface (D046).
Requirements Interview Prep: Consolidation of existing interfaces is required before the 2026-05-20 requirements interview (D061).
Requirements Interview Prep: Preparation of a system architecture diagram is required before the 2026-05-20 requirements interview (D062).
Requirements Interview Prep: Preparation of a data flow diagram is required before the 2026-05-20 requirements interview (D063).
risks: Project Bidding Process: The project is currently in the bidding process (D051). There is a risk of project delay or cancellation if the bid is unsuccessful. (Likelihood: High, Impact: High)

Rushed Requirements: An immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation may be needed on the afternoon the bid is awarded (D053). This poses a risk of rushed or incomplete requirements gathering if not adequately prepared for or if key stakeholders are unavailable, potentially leading to rework or scope creep. (Likelihood: Moderate, Impact: High)
open_questions: What are the specific details of the AI interpretation notification mechanism? (Owned by PM/QA, D003)
Should suspected point layers be split by county/city for management and display purposes? (Owned by PM/QA, D030)
What are the detailed requirements and scope for the legacy MIS system migration, especially regarding data integrity and security compliance? (Owned by PM/QA, D042)
Who are the intended recipients of follow-up notifications, and what is the required notification schedule/cadence? (Owned by PM/QA, D064)
milestones: Project expected to kick off in mid-May (if bidding process successful) (D052).
Immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation scheduled for the afternoon of the bid award day (D053).

Sherry to prepare the data-flow document for A-Bao's review by 2026-05-18 (D056).

system_requirements: The system SHALL create one background task for each AI interpretation run, similar to the CC platform's task list functionality.
The system SHALL issue a notification upon the completion of an AI interpretation task.
The system SHALL accept images for AI interpretation from two sources: a large-scale nationwide batch from Vendor A, and individual user uploads.
Upon completion of AI interpretation, the system SHALL generate and display a list of suspected points for user review.
The system SHALL provide functionality for users to confirm or reject individual suspected points.
The system SHALL allow users to designate confirmed suspected points for monitoring.
When a user places a suspected point under monitoring, the system SHALL require the input of a reason.
When a user places a suspected point under monitoring, the system SHALL require the upload of an associated polygon.
The system SHALL accept large-area polygons as valid inputs for monitoring.
The system SHALL automatically filter out overly small polygons provided for monitoring.
The system SHALL implement a mechanism to prevent duplicate image uploads, given their large file size.
The two-period image comparison feature SHALL NOT retrieve images from the existing image library.
The system SHALL provide tools for users to manually annotate images for two-period image comparison.
The map viewer SHALL be capable of loading historically uploaded imagery.
The map viewer SHALL provide functionality to filter imagery by date.
The map viewer SHALL provide functionality to filter imagery by image-name keyword.
The map viewer SHALL provide functionality to filter imagery by type (satellite, aerial photo, or drone).
The map viewer SHALL display points on the map.
The map viewer SHALL present a 'locate' button when a point is selected or opened.
Pressing the 'locate' button for a point SHALL cause the map to automatically zoom to that point's location.
The map viewer SHALL automatically switch from displaying imagery to displaying points when zoomed out beyond a predefined scale.
In comparison mode, the map view SHALL be locked, preventing user panning.
The system SHALL provide a 'release drag mode' button to re-enable panning in comparison mode.
The system SHALL allow users to adjust the transparency of displayed images.
The system SHALL organize projects hierarchically by year.
The system SHALL enable the creation of suspected points within the context of a project.
Suspected points SHALL require an associated uploaded polygon.
Suspected points MUST NOT require an image upload.
Each year's suspected points SHALL constitute a distinct layer for display (inferred - needs confirmation).
The system SHALL provide a redesigned user interface for the migrated MS file management system.
The core functionality of the migrated MS file management system SHALL be preserved without change.
The system SHALL ensure that the migrated MS file management system complies with information-security requirements.
The system SHALL include a follow-up and reminder feature that supports email notifications (inferred - re-establish legacy functionality).
The follow-up module SHALL allow users to select items for follow-up directly from the suspected points list.
The follow-up module SHALL include a schedule-tracking interface displaying details such as days remaining.
The system SHALL include a follow-up dispatch interface.
Upon completion of a follow-up action, the system SHALL integrate relevant data into the survey-report management module.
The survey-report management module SHALL include fields for time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
data_flows: Images for AI interpretation SHALL flow from either Vendor A's batch imagery or user uploads.
For AI interpretation, images SHALL flow from the image source to the interpretation task execution (on-site workstation), then result output SHALL flow to a monitoring database.
Images for image comparison model SHALL be sent to the client's Access Point (AP), which SHALL then call the on-site workstation to run the model.
After a follow-up action, data SHALL flow to the survey-report management module.
interface_contracts: The 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL include the following fields: Survey Serial Number (string/int), Drawing Number (string), Key Monitoring Zone (string), Village (string), Monitoring Date (date), Monitoring Method (enum: precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), Volume (cubic meters; float/decimal), Area (square meters; float/decimal), Section (string), Lot Number (string), X/Y Coordinates (float, pair), Longitude/Latitude (float, pair), and Approximate Location (string).

constraints.text: The image comparison model MUST run on a dedicated on-site workstation.
Image processing for the image comparison model MUST route through the client's Access Point (AP) to the on-site workstation.
The base map SHALL be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
The map viewer's design MUST reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.
The migration of the legacy MS file management system MUST meet information-security compliance requirements.
The project is expected to kick off in mid-May if the bidding process is successful.
A requirements interview and interface confirmation may be required immediately on the afternoon of bid award.
The 'add suspected point' data interface (referring to figure 2.13) MUST be finished by May 18th.
Existing interfaces (including those owned by Shao-Chien) MUST be organized for the requirements interview by May 20th.
The data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) for A-Bao MUST be prepared by May 18th.
The next meeting to discuss system architecture is scheduled for 2026-05-15.
The first requirements interview is scheduled for 2026-05-20.
Consolidated existing interfaces must be prepared for the requirements interview.
A system architecture diagram must be prepared for the requirements interview.
A data flow diagram must be prepared for the requirements interview.

architectural_decisions.text: The legacy MS file management system WILL be migrated to the new system.

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific technical details of the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion (e.g., protocol, payload, integration points)?
Should the suspected point layers be split by county/city (impacting data organization and mapping)?
What are the specific technical details and scope for the legacy MIS system migration (e.g., data mapping, schema changes, historical data migration strategy, authentication integration)?

stakeholder.business_summary: This meeting clarified requirements for a new system featuring AI-driven image analysis to identify potential issues, along with enhanced map viewing tools and streamlined management of these findings. A critical objective is to migrate our legacy system for improved information security and compliance, with a project kick-off targeted for mid-May if current bidding is successful.
key_impact: Improved efficiency and detection accuracy through automated identification of potential issues using Artificial Intelligence (AI).
Enhanced visual analysis and historical data review capabilities with a new, feature-rich map viewer, making data more accessible and actionable.
Streamlined management and follow-up on identified potential issues, improving accountability and tracking of critical actions.
Modernized system interface and improved data security by migrating the legacy management system, ensuring compliance with current standards.
Better organization and tracking of "suspected points" for more effective oversight and decision-making.
decisions_needing_alignment: Confirmation is needed on the specific details of how users will be notified when AI image analysis tasks are complete.
A decision is required on whether to organize suspected point data layers by county or city.
Further details on the migration process for the legacy management system need to be confirmed.
Clarification is needed regarding who should receive follow-up notifications and on what schedule.
major_unresolved_questions: How exactly will users be notified when the AI finishes analyzing images, and what are the specific notification methods?
Should we organize the identified "suspected points" data within the system by county or by city?
What are the precise plans and details for migrating our old file management system to the new platform?
Who should receive critical follow-up notifications, and what should the timing and frequency of these notifications be?

issues.description: The Engineer view states, 'Re-implement or integrate the existing email-based follow-up/reminder feature (D043).' While D043 confirms the *legacy* system used email, the extracted decisions (D044-D048, D064) for the *new* follow-up module do not specify that the notification mechanism for the new system will also be email-based. This presents an inferred detail (email as the notification mechanism for the new system) as a confirmed fact, overstating certainty.
issues.suggestion: The Engineer view should either rephrase this to reflect that the notification mechanism for the new follow-up module is an open question (referencing D064), or explicitly state that the decision to carry over email notifications to the new system is an assumption that requires confirmation.

review.reviewer_notes: The report generally maintains high quality, with all role outputs largely traceable to extracted decisions. The stakeholder view is clear and concise, and the QA view provides comprehensive acceptance criteria, test scenarios, and edge cases. However, a critical issue was identified in the Engineer view regarding the assumed notification mechanism for the new follow-up feature, which overstates certainty beyond what is explicitly confirmed in the extracted decisions. This needs to be addressed.
